Self::Err> {\n277\t        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n278\t            \"blocks\" => Ok(Self::Blocks),\n279\t            \"parent-child\" => Ok(Self::ParentChild),\n280\t            \"conditional-blocks\" => Ok(Self::ConditionalBlocks),\n281\t            \"waits-for\" => Ok(Self::WaitsFor),\n282\t            \"related\" => Ok(Self::Related),\n283\t            \"discovered-from\" => Ok(Self::DiscoveredFrom),\n284\t            \"replies-to\" => Ok(Self::RepliesTo),\n285\t            \"relates-to\" => Ok(Self::RelatesTo),\n286\t            \"duplicates\" => Ok(Self::Duplicates),\n287\t            \"supersedes\" => Ok(Self::Supersedes),\n288\t            \"caused-by\" => Ok(Self::CausedBy),\n289\t            other => Ok(Self::Custom(other.to_string())),\n290\t        }\n291\t    }\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t/// Audit event type.\n295\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n296\tpub enum EventType {\n297\t    Created,\n298\t    Updated,\n299\t    StatusChanged,\n300\t    PriorityChanged,\n301\t    AssigneeChanged,\n302\t    Commented,\n303\t    Closed,\n304\t    Reopened,\n305\t    DependencyAdded,\n306\t    DependencyRemoved,\n307\t    LabelAdded,\n308\t    LabelRemoved,\n309\t    Compacted,\n310\t    Deleted,\n311\t    Restored,\n312\t    Custom(String),\n313\t}\n314\t\n315\timpl EventType {\n316\t    #[must_use]\n317\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n318\t        match self {\n319\t            Self::Created => \"created\",\n320\t            Self::Updated => \"updated\",\n321\t            Self::StatusChanged => \"status_changed\",\n322\t            Self::PriorityChanged => \"priority_changed\",\n323\t            Self::AssigneeChanged => \"assignee_changed\",\n324\t            Self::Commented => \"commented\",\n325\t            Self::Closed => \"closed\",\n326\t            Self::Reopened => \"reopened\",\n327\t            Self::DependencyAdded => \"dependency_added\",\n328\t            Self::DependencyRemoved => \"dependency_removed\",\n329\t            Self::LabelAdded => \"label_added\",\n330\t            Self::LabelRemoved => \"label_removed\",\n331\t            Self::Compacted => \"compacted\",\n332\t            Self::Deleted => \"deleted\",\n333\t            Self::Restored => \"restored\",\n334\t            Self::Custom(value) => value,\n335\t        }\n336\t    }\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\timpl Serialize for EventType {\n340\t    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {\n341\t        serializer.serialize_str(self.as_str())\n342\t    }\n343\t}\n344\t\n345\timpl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for EventType {\n346\t    fn deserialize<D: serde::Deserializer<'de>>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error> {\n347\t        let value = String::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n348\t        let event_type = match value.as_str() {\n349\t            \"created\" => Self::Created,\n350\t            \"updated\" => Self::Updated,\n351\t            \"status_changed\" => Self::StatusChanged,\n352\t            \"priority_changed\" => Self::PriorityChanged,\n353\t            \"assignee_changed\" => Self::AssigneeChanged,\n354\t            \"commented\" => Self::Commented,\n355\t            \"closed\" => Self::Closed,\n356\t            \"reopened\" => Self::Reopened,\n357\t            \"dependency_added\" => Self::DependencyAdded,\n358\t            \"dependency_removed\" => Self::DependencyRemoved,\n359\t            \"label_added\" => Self::LabelAdded,\n360\t            \"label_removed\" => Self::LabelRemoved,\n361\t            \"compacted\" => Self::Compacted,\n362\t            \"deleted\" => Self::Deleted,\n363\t            \"restored\" => Self::Restored,\n364\t            _ => Self::Custom(value),\n365\t        };\n366\t        Ok(event_type)\n367\t    }\n368\t}\n369\t\n370\t/// The primary issue .iter()\n573\t            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n574\t            .any(|d| {\n575\t                self.source_repo\n576\t                    .as_ref()\n577\t                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n578\t                    .unwrap_or(false)\n579\t            })\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n583\t        use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n584\t        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(self).unwrap_or_default();\n585\t        let hash = Sha256::digest(serialized.as_bytes());\n586\t        format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n587\t    }\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\t/// Relationship between two issues.\n591\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n592\tpub struct Dependency {\n593\t {\n686\t        // This is the exact format br (beads_rust) exports to issues.jsonl\n687\t        let json = r#\"{\n688\t  \"id\": \"bf-1bz\",\n689\t  \"title\": \"Port br-compatible Issue data model with identical Serde attributes (src/model.rs)\",\n690\t  \"description\": \"Port Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType, Dependency, DependencyType, Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n691\t  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n692\t  \"priority\": 0,\n693\t  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n694\t  \"assignee\": \"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\",\n695\t  \"created_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:21:25.206825571Z\",\n696\t  \"created_by\": \"coding\",\n697\t  \"updated_at\": \"phase-1\"]\n702\t}\"#;\n703\t\n704\t        // Deserialize\n705\t        let issue: Issue = serde_json::from_str(json).expect(\"Failed to deserialize br format\");\n706\t\n707\t        // Verify key Ord)]\n#[serde(transparent)]\npub struct Priority(pub i32);\n\nimpl Default for Priority {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::MEDIUM\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Priority {\n    pub const CRITICAL: Self = Self(0);\n    pub const HIGH: Self = Self(1);\n    pub const MEDIUM: Self = Self(2);\n    pub const LOW: Self = Self(3);\n    pub const BACKLOG: Self = Self(4);\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Priority Option<i32>,\n\n    // Messaging\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub sender: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub ephemeral: bool,\n\n    // Context\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub pinned: bool,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub is_template: bool,\n\n    // Relations (for export/display, not always in DB table directly)\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub labels: Vec<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Issue {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: String::new(),\n            content_hash: None,\n            title: String::new(),\n            description: None,\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n            notes: None,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            assignee: None,\n            owner: None,\n            estimated_minutes: None,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            created_by: None,\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            closed_at: None,\n            close_reason: None,\n            closed_by_session: None,\n            due_at: None,\n            defer_until: None,\n            external_ref: None,\n            source_system: None,\n            source_repo: None,\n            deleted_at: None,\n            deleted_by: None,\n            delete_reason: None,\n            original_type: None,\n            compaction_level: None,\n            compacted_at: None,\n            compacted_at_commit: None,\n            original_size: None,\n            sender: None,\n            ephemeral: false,\n            pinned: false,\n            is_template: false,\n            labels: Vec::new(),\n            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n            comments: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Issue {\n    pub fn new(id: String, title: String, source_repo: String) -> Self {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        Issue {\n            id,\n            title,\n            source_repo: Some(source_repo),\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_blocked(&self) -> bool {\n        self.dependencies\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n            .any(|d| {\n                self.source_repo\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n    }\n\n    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n        use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(self).unwrap_or_default();\n        let hash = Sha256::digest(serialized.as_bytes());\n        format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Relationship between two issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Dependency {\n    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n    pub issue_id: String,\n\n    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n    pub depends_on_id: String,\n\n    /// Type of dependency.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// A comment on an issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Comment {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub author: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n    pub body: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Event {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub event_type: EventType,\n    pub actor: String,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub comment: Option<String>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Changes that can be applied to an issue (non-Serde, for internal updates).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueChanges {\n    pub title: Option<String>,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub design: Option<String>,\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub owner: Option<String>,\n    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub defer_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// Filter for listing issues (non-Serde, for queries).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueFilter {\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub limit: Option<usize>,\n    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use chrono::TimeZone;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_issue_roundtrip_from_br_format() {\n        // This is the exact format br (beads_rust) exports to issues.jsonl\n        let json = r#\"{\n  \"id\": \"bf-1bz\",\n  \"title\": \"Port br-compatible Issue data model with identical Serde attributes (src/model.rs)\",\n  \"description\": \"Port Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType, Dependency, DependencyType, Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n  \"priority\": 0,\n  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n  \"assignee\": \"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\",\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:21:25.206825571Z\",\n  \"created_by\": \"coding\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:51:34.458178699Z\",\n  \"source_repo\": \".\",\n  \"compaction_level\": 0,\n  \"original_size\": 0,\n  \"labels\": [\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]\n}\"#;\n\n        // Deserialize\n        let issue: Issue = serde_json::from_str(json).expect(\"Failed to deserialize br format\");\n\n        // Verify key fields\n        assert_eq!(issue.id, \"bf-1bz\");\n        assert_eq!(issue.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(issue.priority, Priority::CRITICAL);\n        assert_eq!(issue.issue_type, IssueType::Task);\n        assert_eq!(issue.assignee.as_deref(), Some(\"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.source_repo.as_deref(), Some(\".\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.compaction_level, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.original_size, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.labels, vec![\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]);\n\n        // Serialize back and verify\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&issue).expect(\"Failed to serialize\");\n        let roundtrip: Issue = serde_json::from_str(&serialized).expect(\"Failed to deserialize roundtrip\");\n\n        // All fields should match after roundtrip\n        assert_eq!(issue, roundtrip);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_compaction_level_serializes_as_zero_when_none() {\n        // bd conformance: compaction_level must serialize as 0, not null\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n513\t    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n514\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n515\t    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n516\t}\n517\t\n518\timpl Default for Issue {\n519\t    fn default() -> Self {\n520\t        Self {\n521\t            id: String::new(),\n522\t            content_hash: None,\n523\t            title: String::new(),\n524\t            description: None,\n525\t            design: None,\n526\t            acceptance_criteria: None,\n527\t            notes: None,\n528\t            status: source_system: None,\n544\t            source_repo: None,\n545\t            deleted_at: None,\n546\t            deleted_by: None,\n547\t            delete_reason: None,\n548\t   sender: None,\n554\t            ephemeral: false,\n555\t            pinned: false,\n556\t            is_template: false,\n557\t            labels: Vec::new(),\n558\t            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n559\t            comments: Vec::new(),\n560\t        }\n561\t    }\n562\t}\n563\t\n564\timpl Issue {\n565\t    /// Compute the deterministic content hash for this issue.\n566\t    ///\n567\t    /// Includes: title, description, design, `acceptance_criteria`, notes,\n568\t    /// status, priority, `issue_type`, assignee, `external_ref`, pinned, `is_template`.\n569\t    /// Excludes: id, timestamps, relations, tombstones.\n570\t    ///\n571\t    /// Delegates to [`crate::util::hash::content_hash`] for the canonical implementation.\n572\t    #[must_use]\n573\t    pub fn compute_content_hash(&self) -> String {\n574\t        crate::util::content_hash(self)\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    /// Compare two issues using sync semantics instead of raw struct equality.\n578\t    ///\n579\t    /// This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make\n580\t    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export\n581\t    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload including\n582\t    /// labels, dependencies, comments, and user-visible timestamps like `due_at`.\n583\t    #[must_use]\n584\t    pub fn sync_equals(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n585\t        if self.id != other.id\n586\t            || self.title != other.title\n587\t            || self.description != other.description\n588\t            || self.design != other.design\n589\t            || self.acceptance_criteria != other.acceptance_criteria\n590\t            || self.notes != other.notes\n591\t            || self.status != other.status\n592\t            || self.priority != other.priority\n593\t            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type\n594\t            || self.assignee != other.assignee\n595\t            || self.owner != other.owner\n596\t            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes\n597\t            || self.created_by != other.created_by\n598\t            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at\n599\t            || self.close_reason != other.close_reason\n600\t            || self.closed_by_session != other.closed_by_session\n601\t            || self.due_at != other.due_at\n602\t            || self.defer_until != other.defer_until\n603\t            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref\n604\t            || self.source_system != other.source_system\n605\t            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo\n606\t            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at\n607\t            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by\n608\t            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason\n609\t            || self.original_type != other.original_type\n610\t            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at\n611\t            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit\n612\t            || self.original_size != other.original_size\n613\t            || self.sender != other.sender\n614\t            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral\n615\t            || self.pinned != other.pinned\n616\t            || self.is_template != other.is_template\n617\t        {\n618\t            return false;\n619\t        }\n620\t\n621\t        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0\n622\t        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {\n623\t            return false;\n624\t        }\n625\t\n626\t        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal\n627\t        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()\n628\t            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()\n629\t        {\n630\t            return false;\n631\t        }\n632\t\n633\t        // Compare labels (order independent)\n634\t        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();\n635\t        self_labels.sort_unstable();\n636\t        self_labels.dedup();\n637\t        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();\n638\t        other_labels.sort_unstable();\n639\t        other_labels.dedup();\n640\t        if self_labels != other_labels {\n641\t            return false;\n642\t        }\n643\t\n644\t        // Compare dependencies (order independent)\n645\t        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();\n646\t        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n647\t            left.issue_id\n648\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n649\t                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n650\t                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n651\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n652\t                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n653\t                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n654\t                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n655\t        });\n656\t        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();\n657\t        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n658\t            left.issue_id\n659\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n660\t                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n661\t                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n662\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n663\t                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n664\t                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n665\t                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n666\t        });\n667\t        if self_deps != other_deps {\n668\t            return false;\n669\t        }\n670\t\n671\t        // Compare comments (order independent)\n672\t        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();\n673\t        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n674\t            left.issue_id\n675\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n676\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n677\t                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n678\t                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n679\t                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n680\t        });\n681\t        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();\n682\t        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n683\t            left.issue_id\n684\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n685\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n686\t                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n687\t                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n688\t                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n689\t        });\n690\t        if self_comments != other_comments {\n691\t            return false;\n692\t        }\n693\t\n694\t        true\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.\n698\t    #[must_use]\n699\t    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {\n700\t        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {\n701\t            return false;\n702\t        }\n703\t\n704\t        let Some(days) = retention_days else {\n705\t            return false;\n706\t        };\n707\t\n708\t        if days == 0 {\n709\t            return false; // Keep forever if 0 (though usually means disabled/immediate, assume safe default)\n710\t        }\n711\t\n712\t        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {\n713\t            return false; // Keep if deletion time is unknown\n714\t        };\n715\t\n716\t        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().\n717\t        // chrono::Duration can handle up to ~292,000 years, but we'll clamp to\n718\t        // something extremely safe for an issue tracker (e.g., 1000 years).\n719\t        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;\n720\t        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);\n721\t        let expiration_time = deleted_at + chrono::Duration::days(days_i64);\n722\t        Utc::now() > expiration_time\n723\t    }\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t/// Epic completion status with child counts.\n727\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\n728\tpub struct EpicStatus {\n729\t    pub epic: Issue,\n730\t    pub total_children: usize,\n731\t    pub closed_children: usize,\n732\t    pub eligible_for_close: bool,\n733\t}\n734\t\n735\t/// Relationship between two issues.\n736\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\n737\tpub struct Dependency {\n738\t    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n739\t    pub issue_id: String,\n740\t\n741\t    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n742\t    pub depends_on_id: String,\n743\t\n744\t    /// Type of dependency.\n745\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n746\t    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n747\t\n748\t    /// Creation timestamp.\n749\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n750\t\n751\t    /// Creator.\n752\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n753\t    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n754\t\n755\t    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n756\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n757\t    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n758\t\n759\t    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n760\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n761\t    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n762\t}\n763\t\n764\t/// A comment on an issue.\n765\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\n766\tpub struct Comment {\n767\t    pub id: i64,\n768\t    pub issue_id: String,\n769\t    pub author: String,\n770\t    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n771\t    pub body: String,\n772\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n773\t}\n774\t\n775\t/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n776\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\n777\tpub struct Event {\n778\t    pub id: i64,\n779\t    pub issue_id: String,\n780\t    pub event_type: EventType,\n781\t    pub actor: String,\n782\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n783\t    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n784\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n785\t    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n786\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n787\t    pub comment: Option<String>,\n788\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t#[cfg(test)]\n792\tmod tests {\n793\t    use super::*;\n794\t    use chrono::TimeZone;\n795\t\n796\t    #[test]\n797\t This module defines the fundamental types used throughout the application:\n//! - `Issue` - The core work item\n//! - `Status` - Issue lifecycle states\n//! - `IssueType` - Categories of issues\n//! - `Dependency` - Relationships between issues\n//! - `Comment` - Issue comments\n//! - `Event` - Audit log entries\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse schemars::JsonSchema;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, None (for bd conformance - bd expects integer, not null)\n#[allow(clippy::ref_option, clippy::trivially_copy_pass_by_ref)]\nfn serialize_compaction_level<S>(value: &Option<i32>, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error>\nwhere\n    S: Serializer,\n{\n    serializer.serialize_i32(value.unwrap_or(0))\n}\n\n/// Issue lifecycle status.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default, JsonSchema)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum Status {\n    #[default]\n    Open,\n    InProgress,\n    Blocked,\n    Deferred,\n    Draft,\n    Closed,\n    #[serde(rename = \"tombstone\")]\n    Tombstone,\n    #[serde(rename = \"pinned\")]\n    Pinned,\n    #[serde(untagged)]\n    Custom(String),\n}\n\nimpl Status {\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Open => \"open\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Blocked => \"blocked\",\n            Self::Deferred => \"deferred\",\n            Self::Draft => \"draft\",\n            Self::Closed => \"closed\",\n            Self::Tombstone => \"tombstone\",\n            Self::Pinned => \"pinned\",\n            Self::Custom(value) => value,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Closed | Self::Tombstone)\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn is_active(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Open | Self::InProgress)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns true if the issue is in draft state (not yet ready for execution).\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn is_draft(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Draft)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Status {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"open\" => Ok(Self::Open),\n            \"in_progress\" | \"inprogress\" => Ok(Self::InProgress),\n            \"blocked\" => Ok(Self::Blocked),\n            \"deferred\" => Ok(Self::Deferred),\n            \"draft\" => Ok(Self::Draft),\n            \"closed\" => Ok(Self::Closed),\n            \"tombstone\" => 4=Backlog).\n#[derive(\n    Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, JsonSchema,\n)]\n#[serde(transparent)]\npub struct Priority(pub i32);\n\nimpl Default for Priority {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::MEDIUM\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Priority {\n    pub const CRITICAL: Self = Self(0);\n    pub const HIGH: Self = Self(1);\n    pub const MEDIUM: Self = Self(2);\n    pub const LOW: Self = Self(3);\n    pub const BACKLOG: Self = Self(4);\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Priority {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"P{}\", self.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl FromStr for Priority {\n    type Err = crate::error::BeadsError;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        let s = s.trim().to_uppercase();\n        let val = s.strip_prefix('P').unwrap_or(&s);\n\n        match val.parse::<i32>() {\n            Ok(p) if (0..=4).contains(&p) => Ok(Self(p)),\n            Ok(p) => Err(crate::error::BeadsError::InvalidPriority { priority: p }),\n            Err(_) => Err(crate::error::BeadsError::InvalidPriority { priority: -1 }),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Issue type    Feature,\n    Epic,\n    Chore,\n    Docs,\n    Question,\n    #[serde(untagged)]\n    Custom(String),\n}\n\nimpl IssueType {\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match           Self::Docs => \"docs\",\n            Self::Question => \"question\",\n            Self::Custom(value) => value,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns true if this is a standard (non-custom) \"question\" => Ok(Self::Question),\n            other => Ok(Self::Custom(other.to_string())),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dependency relationship type.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, => \"replies-to\",\n            Self::RelatesTo => \"relates-to\",\n            Self::Duplicates => \"duplicates\",\n            Self::Supersedes => \"supersedes\",\n            Self::CausedBy => Self::Blocks | Self::ParentChild | Self::ConditionalBlocks | Self::WaitsFor\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub const fn is_blocking(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            Self::Blocks | Self::ParentChild | Self::ConditionalBlocks | Self::WaitsFor\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for DependencyType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) ->          \"caused-by\" => Ok(Self::CausedBy),\n            other => Ok(Self::Custom(other.to_string())),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Audit event type.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum EventType {\n    Created,\n    Updated,\n    StatusChanged,\n    PriorityChanged,\n    AssigneeChanged,\n    Commented,\n    Closed,\n    Reopened,\n    DependencyAdded,\n    DependencyRemoved,\n    LabelAdded,\n    LabelRemoved,\n    Compacted,\n    Deleted,\n    Restored,\n    Custom(String),\n}\n\nimpl EventType {\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Created => \"created\",\n            Self::Updated => \"updated\",\n            Self::StatusChanged => \"status_changed\",\n            Self::PriorityChanged => \"priority_changed\",\n            Self::AssigneeChanged => \"assignee_changed\",\n            Self::Commented => \"commented\",\n            Self::Closed => \"closed\",\n            Self::Reopened => Self::LabelRemoved => \"label_removed\",\n            Self::Compacted => \"compacted\",\n            Self::Deleted => \"deleted\",\n            Self::Restored => \"restored\",\n            Self::Custom(value) => value,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Serialize for EventType {\n    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {\n let value = String::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n        let event_type = match value.as_str() {\n            \"created\" => Self::Created,\n            \"updated\" => Self::Updated,\n            \"status_changed\" => Self::StatusChanged,\n            \"priority_changed\" => Self::PriorityChanged,\n            \"assignee_changed\" => Self::AssigneeChanged,\n            \"commented\" => Self::Commented,\n            \"closed\" => Self::Closed,\n            \"reopened\" => Self::Reopened,\n            \"dependency_added\" => Self::DependencyAdded,\n            \"dependency_removed\" => Self::DependencyRemoved,\n            \"label_added\" => Self::LabelAdded,\n            \"label_removed\" => Self::LabelRemoved,\n            \"compacted\" => Self::Compacted,\n            \"deleted\" => Self::Deleted,\n            \"restored\" => Self::Restored,\n            _ => Self::Custom(value),\n        };\n        Ok(event_type)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl JsonSchema for EventType {\n entity.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\npub struct Issue {\n    /// Unique ID (e.g., \"bd-abc123\").\n    pub id: String,\n\n    /// Content hash for deduplication and sync.\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub content_hash: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Title (1-500 chars).\n    pub title: String,\n\n    /// Detailed description.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Technical design notes.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub design: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Acceptance criteria.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Additional notes.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Workflow status.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub status: Status,\n\n    /// Priority (0=Critical, 4=Backlog).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub priority: Priority,\n\n    /// Issue type (bug, feature, etc.).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub issue_type: IssueType,\n\n    /// Assigned user.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Issue owner.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub owner: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Estimated effort in minutes.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n\n    /// Creation /// Reason for closure.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub close_reason: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Session ID that closed this issue.\n    #[serde(default, JIRA-123).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Source system for imported issues.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub source_system: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Source repository for multi-repo support.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub source_repo: Option<String>,\n\n    // Tombstone fields\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub deleted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub deleted_by: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub delete_reason: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub original_type: Option<String>,\n\n    // Compaction (legacy/compat)\n    // Note: Always serialize compaction_level as integer (0 when None) for bd conformance\n    // bd's Go sql scanner cannot handle NULL for integer columns\n    #[serde(default, serialize_with = \"serialize_compaction_level\")]\n    pub compaction_level: Option<i32>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub compacted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub compacted_at_commit: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub original_size: Option<i32>,\n\n    // Messaging\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub sender: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub ephemeral: bool,\n\n    // Context\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub pinned: bool,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub is_template: bool,\n\n    // Relations (for export/display, not always in DB table directly)\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub labels: Vec<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Issue {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: String::new(),\n            content_hash: None,\n            title: String::new(),\n            description: None,\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n            notes: None,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            assignee: None,\n            owner: None,\n            estimated_minutes: None,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            created_by: None,\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            closed_at: None,\n            close_reason: None,\n            closed_by_session: None,\n            due_at: None,\n            defer_until: None,\n            external_ref: None,\n            source_system: None,\n            source_repo: None,\n            deleted_at: None,\n            deleted_by: None,\n            delete_reason: None,\n            original_type: None,\n            compaction_level: None,\n            compacted_at: None,\n            compacted_at_commit: None,\n            original_size: None,\n            sender: None,\n            ephemeral: false,\n            pinned: false,\n            is_template: false,\n            labels: Vec::new(),\n            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n            comments: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Issue {\n    /// Compute the deterministic content hash for this issue.\n    ///\n    /// Includes: title, description, design, `acceptance_criteria`, notes,\n    /// status, priority, `issue_type`, assignee, `external_ref`, pinned, `is_template`.\n    /// Excludes: id, timestamps, relations, tombstones.\n    ///\n    /// Delegates to [`crate::util::hash::content_hash`] for the canonical implementation.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn compute_content_hash(&self) -> String {\n        crate::util::content_hash(self)\n    }\n\n    /// Compare two issues using sync semantics instead of raw struct equality.\n    ///\n    /// This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make\n    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export\n    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload including\n    /// labels, dependencies, comments, and user-visible timestamps like `due_at`.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn sync_equals(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        if self.id != other.id\n            || self.title != other.title\n            || self.description != other.description\n            || self.design != other.design\n            || self.acceptance_criteria != other.acceptance_criteria\n            || self.notes != other.notes\n            || self.status != other.status\n            || self.priority != other.priority\n            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type\n            || self.assignee != other.assignee\n            || self.owner != other.owner\n            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes\n            || self.created_by != other.created_by\n            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at\n            || self.close_reason != other.close_reason\n            || self.closed_by_session != other.closed_by_session\n            || self.due_at != other.due_at\n            || self.defer_until != other.defer_until\n            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref\n            || self.source_system != other.source_system\n            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo\n            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at\n            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by\n            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason\n            || self.original_type != other.original_type\n            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at\n            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit\n            || self.original_size != other.original_size\n            || self.sender != other.sender\n            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral\n            || self.pinned != other.pinned\n            || self.is_template != other.is_template\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0\n        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal\n        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()\n            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare labels (order independent)\n        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();\n        self_labels.sort_unstable();\n        self_labels.dedup();\n        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();\n        other_labels.sort_unstable();\n        other_labels.dedup();\n        if self_labels != other_labels {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare dependencies (order independent)\n        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();\n        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();\n        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        if self_deps != other_deps {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare comments (order independent)\n        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();\n        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();\n        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        if self_comments != other_comments {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        true\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {\n        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let Some(days) = retention_days else {\n            return false;\n        };\n\n        if days == 0 {\n            return false; // Keep forever if 0 (though usually means disabled/immediate, assume safe default)\n        }\n\n        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {\n            return false; // Keep if deletion time is unknown\n        };\n\n        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().\n        // chrono::Duration can handle up to ~292,000 years, but we'll clamp to\n        // something extremely safe for an issue tracker (e.g., 1000 years).\n        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;\n        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);\n        let expiration_time = deleted_at + chrono::Duration::days(days_i64);\n        Utc::now() > expiration_time\n    }\n}\n\n/// Epic completion status with child counts.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\npub struct EpicStatus {\n    pub epic: Issue,\n    pub total_children: usize,\n    pub closed_children: usize,\n    pub eligible_for_close: bool,\n}\n\n/// Relationship between two issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\npub struct Dependency {\n    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n    pub issue_id: String,\n\n    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n    pub depends_on_id: String,\n\n    /// Type of dependency.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// A comment on an issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\npub struct Comment {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub author: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n    pub body: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, JsonSchema)]\npub struct Event {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub event_type: EventType,\n    pub actor: String,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub comment: Option<String>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use chrono::TimeZone;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn status_custom_roundtrip() {\n        let status: Status = None,\n            title: \"Test Title\".to_string(),\n            description: Some(\"Test Description\".to_string()),\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n default)]\n    pub labels: Vec<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Issue {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: String::new(),\n            content_hash: None,\n            title: String::new(),\n            description: None,\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n            notes: None,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            assignee: None,\n            owner: None,\n            estimated_minutes: None,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            created_by: None,\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            closed_at: None,\n            close_reason: None,\n            closed_by_session: None,\n            due_at: None,\n            defer_until: None,\n            external_ref: None,\n            source_system: None,\n            source_repo: None,\n            deleted_at: None,\n            deleted_by: None,\n            delete_reason: None,\n            original_type: None,\n            compaction_level: None,\n            compacted_at: None,\n            compacted_at_commit: None,\n            original_size: None,\n            sender: None,\n            ephemeral: false,\n            pinned: false,\n            is_template: false,\n            labels: Vec::new(),\n            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n            comments: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Issue {\n    pub fn new(id: String, title: String, source_repo: String) -> Self {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        Issue {\n            id,\n            title,\n            source_repo: Some(source_repo),\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_blocked(&self) -> bool {\n        self.dependencies\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n            .any(|d| {\n                self.source_repo\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n    }\n\n    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n        use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(self).unwrap_or_default();\n        let hash = Sha256::digest(serialized.as_bytes());\n        format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Relationship between two issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Dependency {\n    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n    pub issue_id: String,\n\n    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n    pub depends_on_id: String,\n\n    /// Type of dependency.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// A comment on an issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Comment {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub author: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n    pub body: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Event {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub comment: Option<String>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Changes that can be applied to an issue (non-Serde, for internal updates).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueChanges {\n    pub title: Option<String>,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub design: Option<String>,\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub priority: #[test]\n    fn test_issue_roundtrip_from_br_format() {\n        // This is the exact format br (beads_rust) exports to issues.jsonl\n        let json = r#\"{\n  \"id\": \"bf-1bz\",\n  \"title\": \"Port br-compatible Issue data model with identical Serde attributes (src/model.rs)\",\n  \"description\": \"Port Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType, Dependency, DependencyType, Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n  \"priority\": 0,\n  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n  \"assignee\": \".\",\n  \"compaction_level\": 0,\n  \"original_size\": 0,\n  \"labels\": [\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]\n}\"#;\n\n        // Deserialize\n        let issue: Issue = Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.labels, vec![\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]);\n\n        // Serialize back and verify\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&issue).expect(\"Failed to serialize\");\n        let roundtrip: Issue = serde_json::from_str(&serialized).expect(\"Failed to deserialize roundtrip\");\n\n        // All fields should match after roundtrip\n        assert_eq!(issue, roundtrip);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_compaction_level_serializes_as_zero_when_none() {\n        // bd conformance: compaction_level must serialize as 0, not null\n This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make","+    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export","+    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload including","+    /// labels, dependencies, comments, and user-visible timestamps like `due_at`.","+    #[must_use]","+    pub fn sync_equals(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {","+        if self.id != other.id","+            || self.title != other.title","+            || self.description != other.description","+            || self.design != other.design","+            || self.acceptance_criteria != other.acceptance_criteria","+            || self.notes != other.notes","+            || self.status != other.status","+            || self.priority != other.priority","+            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type","+            || self.assignee != other.assignee","+            || self.owner != other.owner","+            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes","+            || self.created_by != other.created_by","+            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at","+            || self.close_reason != other.close_reason","+            || self.closed_by_session != other.closed_by_session","+            || self.due_at != other.due_at","+            || self.defer_until != other.defer_until","+            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref","+            || self.source_system != other.source_system","+            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo","+            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at","+            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by","+            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason","+            || self.original_type != other.original_type","+            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at","+            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit","+            || self.original_size != other.original_size","+            || self.sender != other.sender","+            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral","+            || self.pinned != other.pinned","+            || self.is_template != other.is_template","+        {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0","+        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal","+        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()","+            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()","+        {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        // Compare labels (order independent)","+        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();","+        self_labels.sort_unstable();","+        self_labels.dedup();","+        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();","+        other_labels.sort_unstable();","+        other_labels.dedup();","+        if self_labels != other_labels {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        // Compare dependencies (order independent)","+        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();","+        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {","+            left.issue_id","+                .cmp(&right.issue_id)","+                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))","+                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))","+                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))","+                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))","+                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))","+                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))","+        });","+        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();","+        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {","+            left.issue_id","+                .cmp(&right.issue_id)","+                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))","+                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))","+                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))","+                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))","+                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))","+                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))","+        });","+        if self_deps != other_deps {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        // Compare comments (order independent)","+        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();","+        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {","+            left.issue_id","+                .cmp(&right.issue_id)","+                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))","+                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))","+                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))","+                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))","+        });","+        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();","+        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {","+            left.issue_id","+                .cmp(&right.issue_id)","+                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))","+                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))","+                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))","+                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))","+        });","+        if self_comments != other_comments {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        true","+    }","+","+    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.","+    #[must_use]","+    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {","+        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        let Some(days) = retention_days else {","+            return false;","+        };","+","+        if days == 0 {","+            return false;","+        }","+","+        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {","+            return false;","+        };","+","+        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().","+        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;","+        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);","+        let expiration_time = deleted_at + bool,\n\n    // Relations (for export/display, not always in DB table directly)\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub labels: Vec<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Issue {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: String::new(),\n            content_hash: None,\n            title: String::new(),\n            description: None,\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n            notes: None,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n sender: None,\n            ephemeral: false,\n            pinned: false,\n            is_template: false,\n            labels: Vec::new(),\n            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n            comments: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Issue {\n    pub fn new(id: String, title: String, source_repo: String) -> Self {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        Issue {\n            id,\n            title,\n            source_repo: Some(source_repo),\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_blocked(&self) -> bool {\n        self.dependencies\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n            .any(|d| {\n                self.source_repo\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n    }\n\n    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n        use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(self).unwrap_or_default();\n        let hash = Sha256::digest(serialized.as_bytes());\n        format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n    }\n\n    /// Compare two issues using sync semantics instead of raw struct equality.\n    ///\n    /// This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make\n    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export\n    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload including\n    /// labels, dependencies, comments, and user-visible timestamps like `due_at`.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn sync_equals(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        if self.id != other.id\n            || self.title != other.title\n            || self.description != other.description\n            || self.design != other.design\n            || self.acceptance_criteria != other.acceptance_criteria\n            || self.notes != other.notes\n            || self.status != other.status\n            || self.priority != other.priority\n            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type\n            || self.assignee != other.assignee\n            || self.owner != other.owner\n            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes\n            || self.created_by != other.created_by\n            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at\n            || self.close_reason != other.close_reason\n            || self.closed_by_session != other.closed_by_session\n            || self.due_at != other.due_at\n            || self.defer_until != other.defer_until\n            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref\n            || self.source_system != other.source_system\n            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo\n            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at\n            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by\n            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason\n            || self.original_type != other.original_type\n            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at\n            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit\n            || self.original_size != other.original_size\n            || self.sender != other.sender\n            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral\n            || self.pinned != other.pinned\n            || self.is_template != other.is_template\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0\n        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal\n        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()\n            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare labels (order independent)\n        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();\n        self_labels.sort_unstable();\n        self_labels.dedup();\n        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();\n        other_labels.sort_unstable();\n        other_labels.dedup();\n        if self_labels != other_labels {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare dependencies (order independent)\n        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();\n        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();\n        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        if self_deps != other_deps {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare comments (order independent)\n        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();\n        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();\n        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        if self_comments != other_comments {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        true\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {\n        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let Some(days) = retention_days else {\n            return false;\n        };\n\n        if days == 0 {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {\n            return false;\n        };\n\n        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().\n        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;\n        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);\n        let expiration_time = deleted_at + chrono::Duration::days(days_i64);\n        Utc::now() > expiration_time\n    }\n}\n\n/// Relationship between two issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Dependency {\n    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n    pub issue_id: String,\n\n    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n    pub depends_on_id: String,\n\n    /// Type of dependency.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// A comment on an issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Comment {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub author: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n    pub body: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Event {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub event_type: EventType,\n    pub actor: String,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub comment: Option<String>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Changes that can be applied to an issue (non-Serde, for internal updates).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueChanges {\n    pub title: Option<String>,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub design: Option<String>,\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub owner: Option<String>,\n    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub defer_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// Filter for listing issues (non-Serde, for queries).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueFilter {\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub limit: Option<usize>,\n    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use chrono::TimeZone;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_issue_roundtrip_from_br_format() {\n        // This is the exact format br (beads_rust) exports to issues.jsonl\n        let json = r#\"{\n  \"id\": \"bf-1bz\",\n  \"title\": \"Port br-compatible Issue data model with identical Serde attributes (src/model.rs)\",\n  \"description\": \"Port Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType, Dependency, DependencyType, Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n  \"priority\": 0,\n  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n  \"assignee\": \"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\",\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:21:25.206825571Z\",\n  \"created_by\": \"coding\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:51:34.458178699Z\",\n  \"source_repo\": \".\",\n  \"compaction_level\": 0,\n  \"original_size\": 0,\n  \"labels\": [\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]\n}\"#;\n\n        // Deserialize\n        let issue: Issue = serde_json::from_str(json).expect(\"Failed to deserialize br format\");\n\n        // Verify key fields\n        assert_eq!(issue.id, \"bf-1bz\");\n        assert_eq!(issue.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(issue.priority, Priority::CRITICAL);\n        assert_eq!(issue.issue_type, IssueType::Task);\n        assert_eq!(issue.assignee.as_deref(), Some(\"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.source_repo.as_deref(), Some(\".\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.compaction_level, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.original_size, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.labels, vec![\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]);\n\n        // Serialize back and verify\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&issue).expect(\"Failed to serialize\");\n        let roundtrip: Issue = serde_json::from_str(&serialized).expect(\"Failed to deserialize roundtrip\");\n\n        // All fields should match after roundtrip\n        assert_eq!(issue, roundtrip);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_compaction_level_serializes_as_zero_when_none() {\n        // bd conformance: compaction_level must serialize as 0, not null\n        let issue = Issue {\n            id: \"test-1\".to_string(),\n            title: \"Test\".to_string(),\n            created_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            updated_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            compaction_level: None,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&issue).unwrap();\n        assert!(json.contains(r#\"\"compaction_level\":0\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"compaction_level\":null\"#));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_empty_vectors_skipped_in_serialization() {\n        let issue = Issue {\n            id: \"test-2\".to_string(),\n            title: \"Test\".to_string(),\n            created_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            updated_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            labels: vec![],\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            comments: vec![],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&issue).unwrap();\n        // Empty vectors should be skipped\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"labels\":\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"dependencies\":\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"comments\":\"#));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_custom_status_roundtrip() {\n        let status: Status =
